text:
To the person at the bus stop who handed me my dropped wallet without a second thought:

Thank you.

You did not wait for me to notice. You did not make a show of it. You simply picked it up and said here, this is yours. Then you went back to looking at your phone.

I do not know your name. I will not see you again. That is fine. Some help is like that: clean, complete, requiring nothing in return.

What I want you to know is that it landed. It mattered. The day had been long and a little hard, and your small action reset something in me. Not dramatically. Just quietly, the way a glass of water helps when you did not realize you were thirsty.

People talk about faith in humanity as if it is a large and fragile thing. I think it is actually small and very sturdy. It lives in moments exactly like the one you gave me.

You were useful to a stranger. That is enough. That is, honestly, a lot.

I hope something equally simple and good finds you soon.

Thank you again.
